spec_match.rationale: The demo now reads the current WICG surface, `navigator.cpuPerformance`, falls back to the legacy draft `navigator.cpu.performance()` method when present, and only then uses the hardwareConcurrency/deviceMemory heuristic. The banner surfaces both the API signal and heuristic fallback.

interactive.rationale: Tier toggle, simulated load sequence with per-resource progress bars and timing, eager/deferred/skipped summary, savings-vs-high-tier readout — a real interactive panel.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a resource manifest, per-tier strategy, simulate-page-load with sequence playback, and a bytes-saved-vs-high summary — all delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Skip-on-low, eager loading, lazy/deferred loading, API-unavailable fallback, and unknown-tier fallback are all represented. Deferred modules now expose an Import now control that runs the progress animation and completes independently of the eager startup sequence.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the required ChromeStatus URL for feature 5189864286978048 and links to the WICG CPU Performance API draft.

design_system.rationale: Badge colors now use shared design tokens and color-mix() against the project variables. Buttons continue to pair local backgrounds with explicit text colors.

summary: The adaptive loader now demonstrates the CPU Performance API directly, documents the fallback path, keeps badge styling inside the design system, includes the required ChromeStatus reference, and lets users trigger deferred imports on demand.
